_about.title: G4 UIFormElement (the FACE form base) + G6 ui-text-field (the first form-associated control)
_about.serves: docs/goals.md §G4 (UIFormElement + control traits) DoD + §G6 ui-text-field DoD (the contenteditable FACE field, applied at the G5 component-rubric bar). UIFormElement is the base ui-checkbox/ui-switch/ui-select/ui-listbox later extend; ui-text-field is its first consumer and the first reuse of the new standards (anatomy / component-packaging / interaction-states / geometry).
_about.method: decomposing-systems, components domain (COMPOSE = OUTSIDE-IN structure: slice groups → file-disjoint leaf slices; REALIZE = INSIDE-OUT actions: the G4+G6 capabilities/acceptance, independent of the structure so they can contradict it). `hosts` crosses them; coverage_check.py --strict is the gate. The order/dep/wave/files/acceptance/gate keys overlay the ordered, file-disjoint fan-out plan onto the coverage-clean structure (the gate ignores non-node/action/host keys).
_about.ground_state: G1 reactive kernel + G2 UIElement/props + G3 template core + G5 ui-button DONE. UIElement (dom/element.ts) ships: connected()/disconnected() hooks, this.effect/listen/emit, attachInternals (the protected `internals` getter — acquired ONCE in the constructor; UIFormElement reuses it, no re-acquire), the render effect (render() is a void no-op when it returns nothing — host-as-grid), the lazy-upgrade dance (property-wins, ADR-0005). props.ts ships finalize()/ReactiveProps/the reflection locks; a subclass's `static props` SHADOWS a base's (no prototype-chain merge today) — so UIFormElement exposes a SPREADABLE `formProps`, it does not rely on inheritance. traits/ ships pressActivation + tabbable (G5); trackUserInvalid is the one G4 trait still UNBUILT. descriptor/ ships the frontmatter schema (BASE_CLASSES already enumerates UIFormElement; face.formAssociated validated boolean) + the contract↔props trip-wire — fleet-wide, reused per-control. The component rubric (docs/rubrics/component.md) + the component-reviewer agent + the browser-truth harness (@vitest/browser + Playwright, npm run test:browser) all EXIST (built at G5) — this milestone RUNS them, it does not build them. shared dimensions.css ships --ui-{height,font,gap}-{sm,md,lg} + [scale]/[density]; --c-focus-ring + --ui-focus-ring-{width,offset} + the motion constants exist; the icon ramp is NOT a shared token (the button inlines --ui-button-icon: calc(18px*scale) locally) — the text-field inlines --ui-text-field-icon the same way, so it needs NO shared/foundation token edit.
_about.decisions_to_ratify: Three design changes this milestone introduces are flagged for ADR ratification (s0, gating like phase1's ADRs): (1) the UIFormElement form-participation MODEL — formAssociated + the hook-driven setFormValue/validity effects installed in connectedCallback (super-wrapped, so the subclass keeps the no-super connected() hook) + the spreadable formProps (no static-props inheritance) + effectiveDisabled = own || formDisabled; (2) the text-field EDITABLE SURFACE — a stable, imperatively-created contenteditable=plaintext-only editor PART (NOT a native <input>, NOT an html`` template — re-rendering a contenteditable subtree destroys the caret), with the value↔surface sync + caret/composition guard; (3) two interaction-states DEVIATIONS the form family needs vs the button-derived standard — the focus ring shows on :focus-within (ALL focus, native text-input parity) not :focus-visible (keyboard-only), and disabled rides the editor + the platform form-disabled channel (effectiveDisabled), NOT host ariaDisabled. The native-<input> exception stays RESERVED for ui-password-field OS masking (plan §12) and is out of scope here.
_about.editable_surface_resolution: RESOLVED — no contradiction with 'no native form elements'. That rule bars native form WIDGETS (<input>/<select>/<textarea>/<button>); a contenteditable region is a generic element with form participation supplied entirely by ElementInternals.setFormValue on the host (the FACE pattern), matching goals.md §G6 ('a contenteditable surface (no native <input>)'). The editor is a control-created light-DOM <div data-part=editor contenteditable=plaintext-only role=textbox aria-multiline=false>, placed in the grid's centre value cell via CSS `order` + the presence-driven :has() grid. It is created ONCE (idempotent guard) and is NEVER re-rendered — the value↔surface sync writes editor.textContent ONLY when the model diverges from the surface (the caret guard: equal ⇒ skip, so a keystroke never resets the caret) and never mid-IME-composition. render() stays the inherited no-op, so the text-field has NO G3 html`` dependency — it parallelizes cleanly with the concurrent G3 template workstream.

_build_legend.now: buildable at the current ground state (G5 done)
_build_legend.prep: serial prerequisite that unblocks a wide fan-out
_build_legend.fan-out: file-disjoint with its wave siblings — one writer per file, parallel-safe
_build_legend.integration: the LONE shared-tree slice (barrels) — every cross-slice edit deferred here, serial
_build_legend.judgment-gate: verified by the component-reviewer's score, not a script

_first_slice: s0 ADR (ratify the three design changes) then s1 UIFormElement (dom/form.ts) — the serial PREP base every text-field slice extends; build it before the wave-C control fan-out.

_waves.A-prep: s0 (ADR, planning-lead) → s1 (form.ts, SERIAL prep) ∥ s2 (track-user-invalid.ts, parallel prep — disjoint, imports only dom)
_waves.C-control: s5 (text-field.ts) ∥ s6 (text-field.css) ∥ s7 (text-field.md)  — file-disjoint fan-out against the fixed LLD interface  [after s1,s2]
_waves.D-control-probes: s8 (text-field.test.ts) ∥ s9 (text-field-geometry.test.ts + text-field-css.test.ts) ∥ s10 (text-field-descriptor.test.ts) ∥ s11 (text-field-*.browser.test.ts)  [after C]
_waves.E-integration: s12 (barrels: dom/index.ts + traits/index.ts + controls/index.ts + component-styles.css + size/tree-shake) — the LONE serial integration slice  [after s1,s2,s5,s6,s7]
_waves.F-score: s14 (component-reviewer scores COMPOSE≥4 AND REALIZE≥4 — the G6 per-control promotion gate)  [after ALL]

_predicted_gates.standing: npm run check && npm test  →  green (every code/probe slice: s1,s2,s3,s4,s5,s6,s8,s9,s10,s12; s7 is a doc but s10 parses it)
_predicted_gates.browser: npm run test:browser  →  Chromium AND WebKit green (s3 the G4 form round-trip proof; s11 the text-field smoke — focus-ring present, geometry px responds to size/scale, forced-colors border survives, value round-trips a real <form>)
_predicted_gates.drift: the contract↔props trip-wire (s10) — text-field.md attributes[] ≡ finalize(UITextFieldElement); a drifted attribute FAILS (negative control)
_predicted_gates.size: npm run size  →  ui-text-field marginal within the family budget; tree-shake clean (importing it drags only it + real deps) (s12)
_predicted_gates.score: component-reviewer verdict COMPOSE≥4 AND REALIZE≥4, zero [gate] fails (s14 — judgment gate)

nodes.label: ADR(s) — ratify the UIFormElement form-participation model + the text-field contenteditable editable surface + the focus-within/disabled-channel deviations
nodes.dep: none (planning-lead authors; gates the build like phase1's ADRs)
nodes.acceptance: ADR(s) record: (1) formAssociated + the hook-driven setFormValue/validity effects installed in a super-wrapped connectedCallback + the spreadable formProps (no static-props inheritance) + effectiveDisabled = own||formDisabled; (2) the stable imperatively-created contenteditable=plaintext-only editor part + the caret/composition guard + render() stays void; (3) the :focus-within (all-focus) ring + the editor/form-disabled disabled channel — both noted as form-family deviations from interaction-states.md (which is button/keyboard-only-derived). harness_checks.py adr exits 0; orchestration-lead accepts before the build proceeds.

nodes.label: dom/form.ts — UIFormElement extends UIElement: static formAssociated=true; the spreadable formProps; the setFormValue/validity effects; the form lifecycle callbacks; the IDL surface
nodes.acceptance: extends UIElement (reuses the protected `internals`, no re-acquire). `static formAssociated = true`. A spreadable `static formProps = { name: string, disabled: boolean(reflect), required: boolean(reflect) }` a subclass spreads into its `static props` (no prototype-chain merge — the base does NOT own a typed `value`; its type/codec is the subclass's). Overridable hooks `formValue(): FormValue` and `validity(): ValidityResult` (a discriminated union `{valid:true} | {valid:false; flags: ValidityStateFlags; message: string}`). connectedCallback() wraps super.connectedCallback() (scope now live) then installs two scope-owned effects: setFormValue(this.formValue()) and applyValidity(this.validity()) → internals.setValidity (clear when valid; flags+message+anchor when invalid); so the subclass keeps the clean no-super connected() hook. Lifecycle: formResetCallback→formReset() (default: value←defaultValue, clear user-invalid), formDisabledCallback(d)→a #formDisabled signal (effectiveDisabled() = own disabled || #formDisabled), formStateRestoreCallback(state,mode)→formStateRestore(state), formAssociatedCallback(form)→a no-op hook. IDL getters delegate to internals: form, name, validity, validationMessage, willValidate, checkValidity(), reportValidity(). Imports only ../reactive + ./element.ts/./props.ts (layering holds).

nodes.label: traits/track-user-invalid.ts — the user-invalid TIMING controller
nodes.dep: G2 UIElement (present); independent of s1 (disjoint file, imports only dom)
nodes.acceptance: trackUserInvalid(host, { invalid: () => boolean }): { interacted: ReadonlySignal<boolean>; release: () => void } — a small CONTROLLER (plan §7 'traits + controllers'). Listens blur/change (via host.listen → the connection AbortSignal) and flips an internal `interacted` signal true on the first interaction; exposes it so the CONTROL composes `userInvalid = interacted.value && invalid()` in its own effect and applies the AX/state via the protected internals (the tabbable/ariaDisabled split — a trait cannot reach protected internals). release() idempotent; scope/abort-owned; zero residue after disconnect; re-arms on reconnect. Imports only ../dom (the one allowed traits→dom direction).

nodes.label: dom/form.test.ts + dom/form.browser.test.ts — UIFormElement probes + the G4 integration proof
nodes.acceptance: jsdom (against an ElementInternals stub where jsdom lacks it): a throwaway UIFormElement subclass — a value write contributes to FormData; validity() non-null sets internals.setValidity; formResetCallback restores defaultValue; formDisabledCallback(true) makes effectiveDisabled reactive; connect→disconnect leaves zero subscribers (the form effects ride the scope). NEGATIVE control: a subclass whose validity() always returns valid never sets an invalid validity. BROWSER (the G4 integration proof, Chromium+WebKit): the throwaway subclass round-trips its value through a real <form> (FormData/submit) and a real internals reports validity.
nodes.gate: npm run check && npm test && npm run test:browser

nodes.label: traits/track-user-invalid.test.ts — the trait contract probe
nodes.acceptance: interacted is false until the first blur/change, then true; userInvalid (composed by the test harness) is false pre-interaction even when invalid; release() is idempotent (safe twice); zero residue after disconnect; re-arms on reconnect. NEGATIVE control: without trackUserInvalid, no blur/change tracking occurs (the timing is the trait's, not free).

nodes.label: controls/text-field/text-field.ts — UITextFieldElement: the contenteditable editor part, value↔surface sync, validity, the typed prop surface, role/disabled/readonly
nodes.acceptance: extends UIFormElement; static props = { ...UIFormElement.formProps (name/disabled/required), value: string (observed, NOT reflected — the initial `value` attribute seeds #defaultValue = getAttribute('value')??'' for reset, native-parity), label: string (→ editor aria-label, the labelling SEAM; the visible label/description/error wrapper is ui-field at G7), placeholder: string, size: enum(sm/md/lg, reflect), readonly: boolean(reflect) }. connected(): create-once (idempotent guard) a light-DOM <div data-part=editor contenteditable=plaintext-only role=textbox aria-multiline=false>, append to self; NO render()/html`` (render() stays the inherited void — re-rendering a contenteditable subtree destroys the caret). SURFACE→MODEL: listen 'input' on the editor → this.value = editor.textContent (host emits 'input'), SKIPPED mid-composition (compositionstart/end guard). MODEL→SURFACE: a scope-owned effect — if this.value !== editor.textContent write editor.textContent (the CARET GUARD: equal ⇒ skip, so a keystroke never resets the caret) and toggle a data-empty attr for the placeholder; never write mid-composition. Enter on the editor → preventDefault (single-line, no newline) + emit 'change'. 'change' also on blur-with-change (track value-at-focus). formValue()=this.value; validity()= required && value==='' ? {valid:false, flags:{valueMissing:true}, message:…, anchor: editor} : {valid:true}. trackUserInvalid(this,{invalid:()=>!this.validity().valid}); an effect applies userInvalid → internals.states.add/delete('user-invalid') + editor aria-invalid. effectiveDisabled() drives the editor: disabled → contenteditable=false + not focusable (no tabindex) + aria-disabled + internals.states.add('disabled'); readonly → contenteditable=false + tabindex=0 (still focusable/selectable) + aria-readonly (still submits). aria-required mirrors required. host.focus() forwards to editor.focus() (label-association + .focus() parity). The HOST carries NO role/aria attribute (form semantics ride internals; the editor carries role=textbox — an internal PART the control owns, not the host). self-defines ui-text-field. NEGATIVE control: a @ts-expect-error proves `size` is the literal union, not string.

nodes.label: controls/text-field/text-field.css — the single sheet: :where token block + @scope styles, host-as-grid, the field frame, h/2 geometry, :focus-within ring, the border-channel states, forced-colors, motion
nodes.dep: s5 (part/slot/state names — fixed by the LLD, so parallel-safe)
nodes.acceptance: single text-field.css (ADR-0003), two sectioned blocks. :where(ui-text-field): declares --ui-text-field-* from --c-{family}-{role} roles + the --ui-{height,font,gap}-{size} ramp; INLINES --ui-text-field-icon: calc(16/18/20px * var(--ui-scale)) per [size] (the content-icon ramp — same local pattern as button.css, NO shared --ui-ind edit); the border channel --ui-text-field-border (idle=outline-variant) / -border-hover (outline) / -border-focus (accent --c-primary) / and the user-invalid border (--c-danger-outline); --ui-text-field-bg (surface role), --ui-text-field-ink (on-surface), --ui-text-field-placeholder (on-surface-variant); [size] repoints the ramp. @scope (ui-text-field): consumes ONLY --ui-text-field-* — host-as-grid (display:inline-grid; presence-driven :has() columns: 1fr | auto 1fr | 1fr auto | auto 1fr auto; placement via `order` on [slot=leading]/[data-part=editor]/[slot=trailing] since the editor is control-injected); block-size off the ramp, padding-block:0; the BORDER-ON-HOST field frame (border + radius on the host so a slotted adornment sits inside the box, §5); value/text edge h/2, slot edge ½(h−icon); column-gap var(--ui-text-field-gap) (the one density-bearing quantity); the editor fills the centre cell (font off the ramp, single-line: white-space:nowrap; overflow hidden; outline:none — the ring is on the host); [data-empty]::before{content:attr(data-placeholder)} for the placeholder; :scope:focus-within draws the SHARED ring (outline: var(--ui-focus-ring-width) solid var(--c-focus-ring); offset) — :focus-within NOT :focus-visible (all-focus, native text-input parity); :hover/:focus-within repoint --ui-text-field-border; :state(user-invalid) → the danger border; :is([disabled], :state(disabled)) → muted + pointer-events:none (hover never lifts); motion gated behind :state(ready) (transition the border/background/colour PAINT only, never geometry), reduced-motion zeroed; a forced-colors block keeps the border + ink + placeholder visible (CanvasText), the ring survives via --c-focus-ring→Highlight. Blocks sectioned for the token-hygiene probe.

nodes.label: controls/text-field/text-field.md — the frontmatter descriptor + /site body (ADR-0004)
nodes.dep: s5 (the settled prop/event surface — fixed by the LLD)
nodes.acceptance: YAML frontmatter: tag ui-text-field; tier control; extends UIFormElement; attributes[] MIRRORS static props (value/label/placeholder/size/disabled/required/readonly/name with type/default/reflect/values); properties (the IDL value getter/setter + the FACE getters as needed); events [input, change]; slots [leading optional, trailing optional] (the editor is a control PART, not a user slot — listed under parts, not slots); parts [editor]; customStates [ready, disabled, user-invalid]; face: formAssociated true, value 'value', validity 'valueMissing'; aria: role textbox (roleSource: editor part — NOT the host), labelSource label/aria-label (ui-field at G7), disabledState editor aria-disabled + the form-disabled channel; keyboard (Enter commits/no-newline; typing edits; the editor is intrinsically focusable, disabled removes it); geometry (control class, block-size off the ramp, padding-block 0, value edge h/2, slot edge ½(h−icon), the border-on-host frame); forcedColors note. Body = the /site prose. REFERENCES anatomy.md/interaction-states.md/geometry.md by their standards (does not restate them).
nodes.gate: npm run check && npm test (s10 parses it)

nodes.label: text-field.test.ts — jsdom behaviour/value/validity/form/disabled/readonly/zero-residue probes
nodes.acceptance: value is a tracked signal (a read inside an effect re-runs on change); surface→model (an editor 'input' sets this.value + emits host 'input'); model→surface caret guard (setting this.value === current textContent does NOT rewrite the editor); programmatic value writes the editor + toggles data-empty; required+empty → validity().valid false (valueMissing), required+filled → valid; the editor carries role=textbox and the HOST carries NO role/aria attribute; effectiveDisabled (own OR formDisabledCallback) → editor contenteditable=false + aria-disabled + :state(disabled); readonly → contenteditable=false + tabindex=0 + aria-readonly; reset restores #defaultValue; Enter emits change + inserts no newline; connect→disconnect zero subscribers + zero live listeners (inspect + the AbortSignal), reconnect re-subscribes clean. NEGATIVE controls per claim (e.g. without the caret guard a keystroke-equal write WOULD rewrite; a bare-string `size` fails @ts-expect-error).

nodes.label: text-field-geometry.test.ts + text-field-css.test.ts — static geometry law + token hygiene
nodes.acceptance: geometry (static, the law): padding-block==0; block-size reads the ramp; the value edge == h/2 and a slot edge == ½(h−icon) (the per-edge formula); 0 < icon ≤ box. css hygiene: every --ui-text-field-* declared in :where(); @scope consumes only --ui-text-field-* (no raw --c-* primitive refs, no role read in @scope); the two blocks are sectioned; a forced-colors block exists; the :focus-within rule reads the shared --c-focus-ring/--ui-focus-ring-* (not a local colour). NEGATIVE control: a planted raw-primitive ref / a block-padding height FAILS.

nodes.label: text-field-descriptor.test.ts — the frontmatter schema + the contract↔props trip-wire
nodes.acceptance: text-field.md frontmatter validateComponentDescriptor → zero failures (extends UIFormElement accepted by BASE_CLASSES; face.formAssociated=true valid); compareDescriptorToProps(parsed, UITextFieldElement.props) → BIJECTION (attributes[] ≡ finalize table: value/label/placeholder/size/disabled/required/readonly/name, types/defaults/reflect/enum-values all agree). NEGATIVE control: a drifted/added/removed attribute or a wrong reflect FAILS (the trip-wire bites).

nodes.label: text-field-*.browser.test.ts — the cross-engine smoke (Chromium AND WebKit)
nodes.acceptance: in Chromium AND WebKit: a real contenteditable editor accepts typed input and this.value tracks it; the value round-trips through a real <form> (FormData) and reset restores; the :focus-within ring is present (outline != none) on editor focus and survives forced-colors; geometry px responds anti-vacuously to [size] sm→md→lg AND [scale] (frame height + font CHANGE) on both the bare and leading-icon variants, value-edge h/2 holds; the field border + ink + placeholder survive forced-colors (do not vanish). NEGATIVE/anti-vacuous: assert the px actually CHANGES (not a vacuous equal-to-self).

nodes.label: barrels — dom/index.ts (+UIFormElement/types) · traits/index.ts (+trackUserInvalid) · controls/index.ts (+text-field) · component-styles.css (@import) + size/tree-shake
nodes.acceptance: the LONE shared-tree slice — every cross-slice barrel edit deferred here, serial: dom/index.ts re-exports UIFormElement + its types (FormValue/ValidityResult); traits/index.ts re-exports trackUserInvalid + its option/return types; controls/index.ts adds `export * from './text-field/text-field.ts'`; component-styles.css adds `@import './controls/text-field/text-field.css'` (after button, before nothing — annotated do-not-reorder). npm run check && npm test green; npm run size shows the ui-text-field marginal within the family budget and tree-shake clean (importing it drags only it + UIFormElement + trackUserInvalid + real deps). If any fleet-wide test enumerates controls/ (component-descriptor-source.test.ts / site-canon.test.ts), update it HERE. The import-layering trip-wire stays green.
nodes.gate: npm run check && npm test && npm run size

nodes.label: rubric score — component-reviewer scores ui-text-field both axes ≥4 (the G6 per-control gate)
nodes.dep: ALL prior slices (scores the complete control)
nodes.acceptance: the EXISTING component-reviewer agent reviews ui-text-field against docs/rubrics/component.md + the contract and returns COMPOSE ≥4 (C1–C5) AND REALIZE ≥4 (C6–C10) with zero [gate] fails — G6 for ui-text-field is declared done. (The rubric + reviewer + browser harness already exist from G5 — this RUNS them.)

actions.label: UIFormElement is form-associated — its value contributes to FormData/submit
the formValue() hook is published to internals.setFormValue, reactively (a scope-owned effect)
the validity() hook drives internals.setValidity (clear/flags+message+anchor) + the willValidate/validationMessage/checkValidity/reportValidity IDL
formResetCallback restores the value to defaultValue (the formReset hook)
formDisabledCallback makes fieldset-disabled reactive — effectiveDisabled = own || formDisabled
formStateRestoreCallback restores the value after navigation/autofill
the spreadable formProps (name/disabled/required) + the IDL getters (form/name/validity/…) — the reusable FACE surface, no static-props inheritance
UIFormElement jsdom probes — value→FormData, validity, reset, formDisabled, zero residue
the G4 integration proof — a throwaway UIFormElement subclass round-trips a value through a real <form> in the browser smoke
user-invalid is suppressed until the first blur/change, then tracks validity (the trait owns the interacted timing)
trackUserInvalid obeys the trait/controller contract — idempotent release, scope/abort-owned, zero residue, re-arms on reconnect
the trait contract is probe-enforced (timing + idempotency + residue + a negative control)
a stable contenteditable=plaintext-only editor PART — the editable surface with NO native <input> and NO html`` re-render (render() stays void)
editor input → this.value + a host 'input' event, with the IME composition guard
programmatic value/reset/restore → editor textContent, with the CARET guard (equal ⇒ skip; never mid-composition) + the data-empty placeholder toggle
value participates in the form (setFormValue via the base) and is a tracked signal
required+empty → valueMissing invalid (anchored on the editor); valid otherwise; reports validity
aria-invalid + :state(user-invalid) appear only AFTER interaction (consumes trackUserInvalid; the control applies the internals)
change is emitted on commit — blur-with-change or Enter (Enter suppresses the newline)
the typed prop surface (value/label/placeholder/size/disabled/required/readonly/name); size is a literal union; the editor carries role=textbox, the host carries no role/aria attribute
effectiveDisabled (own||form) → editor not editable/not focusable/aria-disabled/host inert; readonly → not editable but focusable + still submits
host-as-grid — the presence-driven :has() grid + `order` places [leading? | editor | trailing?]; the editor is the centre value cell (anatomy.md reuse, editor substituted for the label)
Control class — block-size off the ramp, padding-block:0, the border-on-host field frame, value edge h/2, slot edge ½(h−icon), gap = font/2×density (geometry.md / §5 reuse)
the field draws the shared ring on :focus-within (ALL focus, native text-input parity — the flagged deviation from the keyboard-only standard) reading the shared focus tokens

the border-channel state ladder (idle/hover/focus border colour), placeholder ink, disabled muted, the user-invalid danger border; motion gated behind :state(ready), reduced-motion zeroed (interaction-states reuse, border-channel variant)
single text-field.css; @scope consumes only --ui-text-field-*; tokens declared in :where() from roles; forced-colors keeps the border/ink/placeholder visible
the public surface is recorded in text-field.md frontmatter (extends UIFormElement, face.formAssociated=true, value/validity face fields, the editor part)
behaviour/value/validity/form/disabled/readonly/zero-residue are probe-enforced (jsdom) + negative controls
the static geometry law + token hygiene are probe-enforced + a negative control
the frontmatter is schema-valid AND attributes[] ≡ finalize(UITextFieldElement) (the contract↔props trip-wire) + a negative control
the cross-engine smoke (Chromium AND WebKit) — focus ring present/forced-colors-safe, geometry px responds (anti-vacuous), value round-trips a real <form>
ui-text-field is consumable via the barrels (dom/trait exports + controls + component-styles), tree-shake/size clean
the component-reviewer scores both axes ≥4 (the G6 per-control promotion gate)
the three design changes (the UIFormElement model + the contenteditable surface + the focus-within/disabled-channel deviations) are ratified as ADR(s)
